name: Oakwood Bank
description_short: Community bank serving agricultural and business customers across Wisconsin with over 100 years of history, offering personal and commercial banking with local decision-making.
description_long:
Oakwood Bank is a full-service community bank headquartered in the Winona County region of Wisconsin, with branches in Augusta, Eau Claire, Pigeon Falls, and Rollingstone. The institution has operated for over 100 years and was previously known as Pigeon Falls State Bank before rebranding. The bank emphasizes its deep roots in agricultural communities and commitment to personalized, relationship-based banking with local loan decision-making.

Oakwood Bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking products including business and agriculture banking, personal banking services, online banking with mobile check deposit, and specialized digital cash management tools. Their service offerings specifically target farm operations and small businesses, with features like bill pay, ACH payments, payroll management, and accounting software integration. They provide consumer conveniences such as ATM locators, account transfers, and live tech support through their digital banking platform.

The bank distinguishes itself through its explicit focus on agricultural lending and community relationships in a rural Wisconsin market. They actively support local initiatives like the Winona County Dairy Showcase and market themselves as a bank that "knows you by name." Their digital infrastructure includes accounting integration with QuickBooks and Quicken, remote deposit capabilities, and positive pay fraud protection—services typically found at larger regional banks but delivered with emphasized local service.

Oakwood Bank operates as a traditional community bank with no indication of competitive rates, specialized consumer lending products, or digital-only capabilities. Their service fees ($10-$30 per statement cycle for digital services) are standard for full-service banks but may not appeal to cost-conscious consumers. The bank's strength lies primarily in serving established agricultural operations and businesses within their four-county service area rather than offering competitive advantages for broader consumer markets. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online banking with login and account management
Mobile banking app with live tech support
Bill pay and one-time or scheduled transfers
Remote deposit capture for checks via scanner
ACH payments and bill payment processing
pros: Over 100 years of banking history with established local relationships in agricultural communities
QuickBooks and Quicken integration for automated transaction downloads and accounting reconciliation
Remote deposit capability with no limit on number of deposits and faster fund access
Positive Pay fraud protection that verifies checks and notifies of suspicious activity
Mobile banking app with live tech support via chat and mobile check deposit functionality
Specialized cash management services including ACH payments and payroll processing for businesses
Local loan decision-making rather than centralized processing, beneficial for agricultural lending
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to four Wisconsin counties (Augusta, Eau Claire, Pigeon Falls, Rollingstone)
Digital service fees are standard but add up ($10-$30 per statement cycle for various features)
No mention of competitive deposit rates, investment products, or premium account tiers
Website does not disclose APR rates, loan terms, or fee schedules for basic banking services
No indication of offerings for credit building, personal loans, or consumer lending products beyond traditional accounts

contact.address: 40214 S Ekern Avenue, Pigeon Falls, WI 54760

best_for: Agricultural businesses and farm operations in the Winona County region seeking specialized agribusiness lending
Small business owners who benefit from local decision-making and relationship-based banking
Established customers in their service area who value personalized service and community connection over digital-only banking
Businesses needing accounting integration, cash management, and fraud prevention tools
diagnosis: Oakwood Bank is best for agricultural operations, established businesses, and consumers within their four-county Wisconsin service area who prioritize local relationships and personalized service over digital convenience or competitive rates. The main caveat is their strictly regional footprint and lack of competitive advantages in consumer lending, rates, or digital innovation compared to larger regional or national banks.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 40214 S Ekern Avenue, Pigeon Falls, WI 54760
